samples.xml_description: Detect a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Support for continuous input and loop detection. Input signal to the FSM. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Reset signal to initialize the FSM. Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state. One. One state. Zero. Zero state. One. One state. One. One state. One. One state. One. One state. The module implements a Mealy FSM detection circuit. At every positive edge of CLK, detect the sequence Input=10011 and set the MATCH signal to 1, for example, the sequence 10011 occurring at the last 5 bits of the input sequence. When the sequence 10011 is detected, the MATCH signal is 1 at the same time as the last occurrence of IN=1. Reset the MATCH and STATE when RST is high. If the sequence 10011 occurs for more than five consecutive bits, detect the sequence for the number of bits and set the MATCH signal to 1 for the same number of clock cycles. <?xml-fragment attribute="value"?> states are used for the FSM detection.
